picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: The Cleveland Cavaliers are in a crucial must-win situation, down 0-2 in the Eastern Conference Finals and returning to their home court. Cleveland boasts a strong 6-1 home playoff record and has historically shown a significant bounce-back ability, winning their last nine home games following a loss. Both teams report a clean injury sheet for Game 3, ensuring the Cavaliers' full roster, including Donovan Mitchell and James Harden, will be available and highly motivated to secure a victory. This combination of home-court advantage, desperation, and historical performance provides a strong edge for the Cavaliers to win outright.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: The New York Knicks are currently on an impressive nine-game playoff winning streak, demonstrating exceptional form and cohesion. They have also performed well on the road in the postseason, holding a 4-1 record away from home. The Knicks have covered the spread in two of their last five head-to-head matchups against the Cavaliers, including both games in this series. With a healthy roster and a strong ATS record this season (49-40), the Knicks are well-positioned to at least keep this game close, if not win it outright, making the +2.5 spread a high-confidence pick.

picks.game: New York Knicks vs Cleveland Cavaliers
picks.rationale: Eastern Conference Finals games are typically characterized by heightened defensive intensity, often leading to lower-scoring affairs. Historically, seven of the last eight home games between the Cavaliers and the Knicks have gone Under the total points line. The Knicks possess an elite playoff defense, ranking first in the league by allowing only 100.6 points per game this postseason. The Cavaliers' offense also struggled in Game 2, shooting just 39.0% from the field and 26.0% from three, suggesting a continued challenge against New York's stout defense.

The Montreal Canadiens have demonstrated a significant edge over the Carolina Hurricanes this season, winning their last five head-to-head matchups, including a decisive 6-2 victory in Game 1 of this series. Despite Carolina's strong underlying possession metrics, Montreal has consistently found ways to win the 'high-danger chance' battle and boasts excellent goaltending from Jakub Dobes against the Hurricanes. Furthermore, sharp money indicators show that 91% of the money is on the Canadiens, despite only 36% of bets, suggesting professional bettors see value in Montreal as an underdog. This combination of recent dominance, strategic advantage, and sharp betting action makes Montreal +1.5 a high-confidence pick.

Game 1 of this Eastern Conference Final saw a total of eight goals, easily cashing the Over on a lower line. While the verified total has adjusted to 6.5, there's significant public and sharp money consistently on the Over at previous totals of 5.5 and 6, indicating a market expectation for continued high scoring. Both teams possess offensive talent, and while Carolina will aim to tighten defensively, their aggressive offensive zone time and shot attempts often lead to chances, which Montreal has proven capable of converting. Given the offensive outburst in Game 1 and the market's movement towards a higher total, the Over 6.5 at plus money offers strong value.

Juraj Slafkovsky delivered a standout performance in Game 1, tallying three points (two goals, one assist) and significantly contributing to his line's five-on-five possession. He has been in exceptional form recently, averaging an elite 4.06 points per 60 minutes over his last six games. Slafkovsky is an active member of the Montreal Canadiens' top line, playing alongside Nick Suzuki and Cole Caufield, a trio that consistently generates offensive opportunities. His recent surge in production and pivotal role in Montreal's offense make him a strong candidate to record at least one point in Game 2.

The Carolina Hurricanes, despite their Game 1 loss, finished the regular season as the top team in the Eastern Conference with a 53-22-7 record and swept their first two playoff series. They are a fully healthy squad with dominant underlying metrics, leading the NHL in offensive zone time and 5-on-5 shot attempts percentage. Coming off a surprising 6-2 defeat at home, Carolina will be highly motivated to even the series on their home ice, a common bounce-back scenario for elite teams in the playoffs. Their overall season-long dominance and a strong desire for redemption make the Hurricanes moneyline a high-confidence pick.

picks.pick: Sean Walker Over 1.5 Shots on Goal
picks.rationale: Carolina Hurricanes defenseman Sean Walker has a compelling trend of exceeding 1.5 shots on goal following a loss, achieving this in all five such instances and averaging 3.4 shots per game. With the Hurricanes coming off a 6-2 defeat in Game 1, they are expected to increase their offensive pressure and direct more pucks towards the net. Walker is an active and confirmed player for the Carolina Hurricanes, projected to be in the lineup for Game 2. This strong situational performance, coupled with the team's anticipated offensive push, makes this a high-confidence player prop.

The Chicago Cubs are in a significant slump, having lost six consecutive games and struggling mightily at the plate, scoring two runs or fewer in eight of their last twelve outings. Despite lineup changes, their offensive woes are deep-seated. Houston's starter, Kai-Wei Teng, has been effective in his recent starts, including a five-scoreless-inning performance against the Rangers. While the Astros have a poor road record, their offense features dangerous hitters like Yordan Alvarez. Given the Cubs' current struggles and the value offered at +136, the Astros moneyline presents a high-confidence opportunity.

The Atlanta Braves are a dominant team with a 36-16 record and a red-hot offense, having scored 40 runs in their last six victories. They face Washington Nationals starter Jake Irvin, who has struggled with a 5.79 ERA and a 1.48 WHIP, and is prone to allowing hard contact. In contrast, Braves starter Grant Holmes has been reliable with a 3-1 record and a 3.80 ERA. The Braves also boast one of MLB's most effective bullpens (3.08 ERA, 1.13 WHIP), providing a significant late-game advantage. This pitching and offensive mismatch, combined with the Braves playing at home, creates a strong edge for Atlanta.

The Milwaukee Brewers boast a strong pitching staff, with Jacob Misiorowski having not allowed a run in May and Kyle Harrison posting an impressive 1.77 ERA. This rotation depth is a significant advantage. Conversely, the Los Angeles Dodgers' bullpen is severely compromised with key relievers Edwin Diaz and Evan Phillips missing, making them vulnerable in late innings. Additionally, Dodgers infielder Max Muncy is injured, further weakening their lineup. With the Brewers playing at home and their offense showing good form, they are well-positioned to capitalize on the Dodgers' pitching woes.

The San Diego Padres are coming off a convincing 7-3 victory over the Athletics yesterday, where they also covered the -1.5 spread. This win continues a strong head-to-head trend, as the Padres have won four of their last five matchups against the Athletics, covering the runline in four of those games. Despite some key injuries, Padres starter Lucas Giolito had a solid first outing for the team, and their offense, featuring Miguel Andujar and Xander Bogaerts, has proven capable. With home-field advantage and recent momentum, the Padres are in a good position to secure another comfortable win.

Both starting pitchers in this doubleheader opener have shown vulnerability against their opponent. Cincinnati's Chris Paddack carries a 7.07 ERA and previously allowed five runs in less than five innings against the Cardinals this season. St. Louis's Leahy, while typically a reliever, has a 6.30 ERA in his career appearances against the Reds. With a doubleheader taxing both bullpens and key offensive players like Elly De La Cruz for the Reds and Alec Burleson for the Cardinals performing well, a high-scoring affair is anticipated. The combination of struggling starters and bullpen fatigue points to the Over 9.5 being a strong play.

San Jose Earthquakes boast a significantly better overall record this season (9-2-3) compared to Portland Timbers (4-2-7). Furthermore, San Jose has been dominant in recent head-to-head matchups, remaining unbeaten in their last three encounters with Portland, which includes a 1-1 draw in their most recent game. While Portland has secured two consecutive home wins, San Jose's superior season form, with 6 wins, 2 losses, and 2 draws in their last 10 league games, provides a strong foundation for an away victory or at least a draw, making the +150 moneyline a valuable play.

FC Dallas carries strong momentum into this match, having secured two consecutive MLS road wins, including a recent 3-2 victory. In contrast, Colorado Rapids have struggled significantly at home, experiencing back-to-back MLS home losses. Dallas also demonstrates superior recent form with a 5-3-2 record in their last 10 league games, outperforming Colorado's 3-6-1 record. The last head-to-head encounter saw Dallas claim a decisive 3-1 home win, further supporting their potential for an upset on the road.

The LA Galaxy have shown a strong tendency for low-scoring games at home, with their last 10 home matches all finishing with Under 3.5 goals. This trend is further reinforced by the head-to-head history between LA Galaxy and Houston Dynamo, where 9 out of their last 10 encounters also went Under 3.5 goals. Despite the market favoring the Over 2.5, these consistent historical patterns, especially the LA Galaxy's home performance, indicate significant value in betting the Under 2.5 goals at plus money.

While Chicago Fire are in better recent form, the head-to-head history between these two teams suggests a tendency towards lower-scoring affairs, with 53% of their last 10 H2H matches going Under 3.5 goals. Chicago's average goals scored (2.1) and conceded (1.3) in their last 10 league games, combined with Toronto's 1.8 goals scored per game, point to a game that is likely to stay below the 3.5 goal mark. The -165 odds for the Under 3.5, while juiced, align with both historical and recent scoring trends for both teams.

picks.game: Real Salt Lake @ Minnesota United FC
picks.rationale: Real Salt Lake enters this match in excellent offensive form, having scored two or more goals in nine of their last 12 contests. Their overall season record of 8-1-4 also reflects a strong attacking presence within the league. Despite playing away from home, RSL's consistent goal-scoring ability, combined with the market heavily favoring the Over 2.5 at -165, suggests a high-scoring affair. This pick aligns with Real Salt Lake's recent offensive output and the implied expectation of goals from the oddsmakers.

Assem Ghanem maintains an undefeated professional record of 7-0, showcasing consistent success throughout his career. He recently secured a dominant unanimous decision victory in May 2025, advancing in the PFL MENA Featherweight Tournament and demonstrating his ability to control fights. Mohammad Fahmi, despite a respectable 4-1 record, suffered his first professional loss via D'arce Choke in his last fight in December 2025. Ghanem's perfect record and recent tournament success indicate strong form and a high level of confidence coming into this bout. Given Ghanem's undefeated status and recent dominant performance against Fahmi's recent loss, Ghanem at +108 offers excellent value as a high-confidence underdog pick.
